title.en: Link Aggregation: How Many Links Become One (Clause 6, Part 1)
title.zh: 链路聚合：多条链路如何合而为一（第 6 章，上篇）

blocks.en: IEEE Std 802.1AX-2020 defines an optional Link Aggregation sublayer that lets a system bundle one or more point-to-point links into a single Link Aggregation Group (LAG). The whole point: an Aggregator Client (the higher-layer entity that hands frames down, such as a bridge or an IP stack) gets to treat the LAG as if it were one ordinary link. Clause 6.1.1 lists the goals this buys you — more bandwidth (capacities add up), higher availability (one link can fail without taking the group down), load sharing, automatic configuration ('if a set of links can aggregate, they will'), rapid reconfiguration (milliseconds for link-down, ~1 s for link-up), deterministic behavior, low risk of frame duplication or misordering, no change to frame formats, and backwards compatibility with devices that have never heard of aggregation. The sublayer rides on top of links offering the IEEE 802.1AC Internal Sublayer Service (ISS) — the standard internal hand-off interface between MAC-layer sublayers.
blocks.zh: IEEE Std 802.1AX-2020 定义了一个可选的链路聚合（Link Aggregation）子层，它把一条或多条点到点链路捆绑成单一的链路聚合组（LAG，Link Aggregation Group）。其核心目的是：让聚合器客户端（Aggregator Client，即向下递交帧的上层实体，例如网桥或 IP 协议栈）能够把整个 LAG 当作一条普通链路来对待。6.1.1 列出了由此带来的目标——更高带宽（各链路容量相加）、更高可用性（单条链路失效不会拖垮整组）、负载分担、自动配置（“能聚合的链路集合就会自动聚合”）、快速重配置（链路掉线约毫秒级，链路上线约 1 秒）、确定性行为、极低的帧重复或乱序风险、不改变帧格式，以及与从未支持聚合的设备保持向后兼容。该子层运行在提供 IEEE 802.1AC 内部子层服务（ISS，Internal Sublayer Service，即 MAC 层子层之间的标准内部交接接口）的链路之上。

blocks.en: Where does it sit? Clause 6.1.2 positions Link Aggregation as a sublayer just above the MAC. Its upper boundary offers an ISS to Aggregator Clients (one per Aggregator Port); its lower boundary consumes an ISS from each Aggregation Port (one per underlying MAC). You can aggregate some ports of a system and leave others alone — a conformant device need not be able to aggregate every port. Earlier editions placed it inside IEEE 802.3; the 2014 revision moved it above the MAC to match industry practice, without breaking interoperability.
blocks.zh: 它位于何处？6.1.2 把链路聚合定位为紧贴 MAC 之上的一个子层。其上边界向聚合器客户端提供 ISS（每个聚合器端口一个）；其下边界则消费来自每个聚合端口（Aggregation Port，每个底层 MAC 一个）的 ISS。你可以只聚合系统的部分端口，其余端口照常独立工作——符合标准的设备并不要求能聚合每一个端口。早期版本曾把它放在 IEEE 802.3 内部，2014 版将其上移到 MAC 之上以贴合业界实践，且未破坏互操作性。

blocks.en: Clause 6.1.3 introduces a reusable building block: the Protocol Parser/Multiplexer. It separates a specific control protocol's frames out of an incoming ISS stream and merges that protocol's outgoing frames back into the stream. It has three ISS interfaces: a DownPort (toward the lower layers), a DataPort (toward higher layers / regular data), and a ControlPort (toward the control-protocol engine). On receive, a single function — IsControlFrame — decides whether a frame is control traffic (up the ControlPort) or data (up the DataPort); on transmit it just passes everything straight through to the DownPort. This standard uses three customizations of it: the LLDP, LACP, and DRCP Parser/Multiplexers. LACP stands for Link Aggregation Control Protocol; DRCP for Distributed Relay Control Protocol; LLDP for Link Layer Discovery Protocol.
blocks.zh: 6.1.3 引入了一个可复用的构件：协议解析/复用器（Protocol Parser/Multiplexer）。它从进入的 ISS 流中把某个特定控制协议的帧分离出来，并把该协议要发出的帧重新合并回流中。它有三个 ISS 接口：DownPort（朝向下层）、DataPort（朝向上层/常规数据）和 ControlPort（朝向控制协议引擎）。在接收方向，单一函数 IsControlFrame 判定一帧是控制流量（经 ControlPort 上送）还是数据（经 DataPort 上送）；在发送方向，它只是把一切原样透传到 DownPort。本标准对它做了三种定制：LLDP、LACP 和 DRCP 解析/复用器。LACP 指链路聚合控制协议（Link Aggregation Control Protocol），DRCP 指分布式中继控制协议（Distributed Relay Control Protocol），LLDP 指链路层发现协议（Link Layer Discovery Protocol）。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 460 250" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><rect x="150" y="10" width="160" height="34" rx="4"/><text x="230" y="32" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle">Aggregator Client</text><rect x="60" y="70" width="340" height="100" rx="4"/><text x="230" y="86" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Link Aggregation sublayer</text><rect x="80" y="96" width="300" height="30" rx="3"/><text x="230" y="115" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">Aggregator (Collect + Distribute)</text><rect x="80" y="132" width="300" height="28" rx="3"/><text x="230" y="150" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Aggregation Control / LACP</text><rect x="70" y="200" width="80" height="30" rx="3"/><rect x="190" y="200" width="80" height="30" rx="3"/><rect x="310" y="200" width="80" height="30" rx="3"/><text x="110" y="219" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">MAC</text><text x="230" y="219" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">MAC</text><text x="350" y="219" fill="currentColor" stroke="none" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle">MAC</text><line x1="230" y1="44" x2="230" y2="70"/><line x1="110" y1="170" x2="110" y2="200"/><line x1="230" y1="170" x2="230" y2="200"/><line x1="350" y1="170" x2="350" y2="200"/></svg>

caption.en: One Aggregator Client sees a single ISS; below it the Link Aggregation sublayer spreads frames across several Aggregation Ports, each backed by a MAC (Clause 6.1.2, Figure 6-1 and Figure 6-3).
caption.zh: 一个聚合器客户端只看到单一的 ISS；其下方的链路聚合子层把帧分散到若干聚合端口，每个端口由一个 MAC 支撑（见 6.1.2，图 6-1 与图 6-3）。

blocks.en: Now the operational machinery (Clause 6.2). The sublayer's principles (6.2.1) are simple: an Aggregator Client talks through exactly one Aggregator at a time, the Aggregator owns the job of spreading transmit frames across ports and collecting received frames back, and a given Aggregation Port attaches to at most one Aggregator. Frame ordering matters for a 'conversation' (a sequence of related frames). The rule that keeps order intact: the Frame Distributor sends all frames of one conversation down a single link, and since one link cannot reorder its own frames, order is preserved end to end. Each Aggregation Port and each Aggregator carries its own MAC address (used as the source address for LACP and Marker exchanges, and as the LAG's address from the client's view). No particular distribution algorithm is mandated.
blocks.zh: 接下来是运行机制（6.2）。子层的原则（6.2.1）很简单：一个聚合器客户端在任一时刻只通过一个聚合器通信，聚合器负责把发送帧分散到各端口、再把接收帧收集回来，而某个聚合端口在任一时刻至多挂接到一个聚合器。帧顺序对“会话（conversation，即一串相关帧）”很重要。保持顺序的规则是：帧分发器（Frame Distributor）把同一会话的所有帧从同一条链路发出，而单条链路不会把自己的帧弄乱序，因此端到端的顺序得以保持。每个聚合端口和每个聚合器都带有各自的 MAC 地址（用作 LACP 与标记协议交换的源地址，并从客户端视角充当 LAG 的地址）。标准不强制采用任何特定的分发算法。

blocks.en:
flowchart LR
  AC[Aggregator Client] -->|frames| FD[Frame Distributor]
  FD -->|conversation X| P1[Port 1]
  FD -->|conversation Y| P2[Port 2]
  P1 --> FC[Frame Collector]
  P2 --> FC
  FC -->|in receive order| AC
blocks.zh:
flowchart LR
  AC[聚合器客户端] -->|帧| FD[帧分发器]
  FD -->|会话 X| P1[端口 1]
  FD -->|会话 Y| P2[端口 2]
  P1 --> FC[帧收集器]
  P2 --> FC
  FC -->|按接收顺序| AC

blocks.en: The two halves of the Aggregator (6.2.2 names the ISS interface prefixes — Agg:, AggMuxN:, CtrlMuxN:, DataMuxN:, MacN:). The Frame Collector (6.2.3) takes incoming frames from each port and delivers them to the client in receive order; it never reorders, and to avoid stalling a moved conversation it must deliver or discard within CollectorMaxDelay. The Frame Distributor (6.2.4) picks the outgoing link per its distribution algorithm, never duplicating or misordering a conversation, and adding nothing to the frame. Two optional helpers support the Marker protocol: the Marker Generator/Receiver (6.2.5) injects a Marker PDU to flush a conversation before moving it, and the Marker Responder (6.2.6) — mandatory to implement — echoes a Marker Response back out the same port. (PDU = Protocol Data Unit.)
blocks.zh: 聚合器的两个组成部分（6.2.2 命名了各 ISS 接口前缀——Agg:、AggMuxN:、CtrlMuxN:、DataMuxN:、MacN:）。帧收集器（Frame Collector，6.2.3）从各端口取入接收帧，并按接收顺序递交给客户端；它从不重排序，且为避免被搬移的会话卡住，必须在 CollectorMaxDelay 时间内完成递交或丢弃。帧分发器（Frame Distributor，6.2.4）按其分发算法选择发送链路，绝不重复或打乱一个会话，也不向帧添加任何内容。两个可选辅助件支撑标记（Marker）协议：标记生成器/接收器（6.2.5）在搬移会话前注入一个 Marker PDU 以清空该会话，而标记响应器（Marker Responder，6.2.6，强制实现）则把 Marker 响应从同一端口原路回送。（PDU 即协议数据单元，Protocol Data Unit。）

blocks.en: Two demultiplexers sit between the Aggregator and the wire. The Aggregator Parser/Multiplexer (6.2.7) is per-port: on transmit it only passes Frame-Distributor traffic when the port state is Distributing (Marker traffic always passes); on receive it routes Marker Requests, Marker Responses, and other MAC PDUs to the right consumer, passing data up only when the port is Collecting. Below that, the LACP Parser/Multiplexer (6.2.9) — one per port, a customization of the 6.1.3 building block — peels off LACPDUs (its IsControlFrame matches the slow-protocols EtherType, the LACP subtype, and length >= 60 octets) and hands everything else to the Aggregator.
blocks.zh: 聚合器与线路之间有两个解复用器。聚合器解析/复用器（Aggregator Parser/Multiplexer，6.2.7）按端口设置：在发送方向，仅当端口处于 Distributing 状态时才放行帧分发器的流量（标记流量始终放行）；在接收方向，它把 Marker 请求、Marker 响应和其他 MAC PDU 路由给正确的消费者，仅当端口处于 Collecting 时才把数据上送。其下方是 LACP 解析/复用器（6.2.9，每端口一个，是 6.1.3 构件的定制）——它剥离出 LACPDU（其 IsControlFrame 匹配慢协议 EtherType、LACP 子类型以及长度 >= 60 字节），其余一律交给聚合器。

blocks.en: The Aggregator itself (6.2.8) bundles one Frame Collection function, one Frame Distribution function, and one or more Aggregator Parser/Multiplexers for a single LAG; it carries one MAC address and presents one ISS to the client. It is 'available' (its MAC_Operational is TRUE) when it has at least one attached port, has not been administratively disabled, and at least one attached port is both Collecting and Distributing. Addressing (6.2.10) closes out 6.2: every MAC has an individual address; an Aggregator's address may equal one of its ports' addresses or be distinct; LACP/Marker frames use the transmitting Aggregation Port's address as source (SA) and a group destination (DA) chosen to limit how far the control frames travel — the Slow_Protocols_Multicast address (01-80-C2-00-00-02) is the default, never forwarded by any bridge, so it reaches only the directly attached neighbor.
blocks.zh: 聚合器本身（6.2.8）为单个 LAG 捆绑了一个帧收集功能、一个帧分发功能，以及一个或多个聚合器解析/复用器；它带有一个 MAC 地址，并向客户端提供一个 ISS。当它满足以下条件时即为“可用”（其 MAC_Operational 为 TRUE）：至少挂接了一个端口、未被管理性禁用，且至少一个挂接端口同时处于 Collecting 与 Distributing。寻址（6.2.10）为 6.2 收尾：每个 MAC 都有一个独立地址；聚合器的地址可与其某个端口的地址相同，也可独立；LACP/标记帧以发送聚合端口的地址作源地址（SA），以选定的组目的地址（DA）限定控制帧的传播范围——默认采用慢协议组播地址（Slow_Protocols_Multicast，01-80-C2-00-00-02），任何网桥都不转发它，因此只能到达直连邻居。

blocks.en: Clause 6.3 is the brain: Link Aggregation Control. For each port it maintains config, exchanges that config with the peer to allocate the link to a LAG, attaches/detaches the port to the LAG's Aggregator, and enables/disables collection and distribution. Its character (6.3.1) is Automatic, Continuous, Deterministic, Controllable, Compatible, and Rapid — convergence in just three LACPDUs, no timer dependence. To do this it needs identities: the System Identifier (6.3.2) = System Priority concatenated with the system MAC address (8 octets, used to spot loopback wiring); the Aggregator gets a unique MAC and integer id (6.3.3); each port gets a Port Identifier = Port Priority + Port Number, never zero (6.3.4); and a Key (6.3.5) — a 16-bit integer summarizing which ports can aggregate together. Each port and Aggregator has both an administrative Key (set by management) and an operational Key (in active use); ports sharing an operational Key may aggregate, and a port can only attach to an Aggregator with the same operational Key. A link that nothing else can aggregate with is a Solitary link.
blocks.zh: 6.3 是大脑：链路聚合控制（Link Aggregation Control）。它为每个端口维护配置、与对端交换配置以把链路分配到某个 LAG、把端口挂接到/脱离该 LAG 的聚合器，并启用/禁用收集与分发。其特性（6.3.1）为：自动、连续、确定、可控、兼容、快速——仅需三个 LACPDU 即可收敛，不依赖定时器。为此它需要若干标识：系统标识符（System Identifier，6.3.2）= 系统优先级与系统 MAC 地址拼接（共 8 字节，用于发现环回接线）；聚合器获得唯一的 MAC 与整数标识（6.3.3）；每个端口获得端口标识符（Port Identifier）= 端口优先级 + 端口编号，且永不为零（6.3.4）；还有一个键（Key，6.3.5）——一个 16 位整数，概括哪些端口可相互聚合。每个端口和聚合器都同时拥有管理键（由管理设置）与运行键（实际生效）；运行键相同的端口才可聚合，且端口只能挂接到运行键相同的聚合器。无法与任何其他链路聚合的链路称为孤立链路（Solitary link）。

blocks.en:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Select: derive LAG ID (6.3.7)
  Select --> Agree: peer confirms LAG (6.3.8)
  Agree --> Attach: bind to compatible Aggregator (6.3.9)
  Attach --> Ready: signal ready, no frames in flight (6.3.10)
  Ready --> Active: enable Collector then Distributor (6.3.11)
  Active --> Detach: LAG membership changed (6.3.13, 6.3.14)
  Detach --> Select
blocks.zh:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> 选择: 推导 LAG ID (6.3.7)
  选择 --> 达成一致: 对端确认 LAG (6.3.8)
  达成一致 --> 挂接: 绑定到兼容聚合器 (6.3.9)
  挂接 --> 就绪: 发出就绪信号，无在途帧 (6.3.10)
  就绪 --> 激活: 先启用收集器再启用分发器 (6.3.11)
  激活 --> 脱离: LAG 成员关系变化 (6.3.13, 6.3.14)
  脱离 --> 选择

summary.en: Coverage map: the full lifecycle clauses 6.3.6 through 6.3.16
summary.zh: 覆盖地图：6.3.6 至 6.3.16 的完整生命周期

blocks.en: Tracing one link from cold start to teardown, with exact clause numbers: 6.3.6 Link Aggregation Group identification — a LAG is either Aggregateable links sharing a System pair and operational Key, or a Solitary link; the LAG ID is built (6.3.6.1) as [(SKP),(TLQ)] from the operational System Identifiers, Keys, and (for Solitary links) Port Identifiers, and written in a canonical hex convention (6.3.6.2). 6.3.7 Selecting a LAG — each port is placed in the LAG named by its LAG ID; selection is briefly delayed to absorb peer information, falling back to administrative defaults if none arrives. 6.3.8 Agreeing on a LAG — both ends must agree on the LAG ID before frames flow; LACP can short-circuit this for a known-Solitary link by clearing the Aggregation bit in the LACPDU Actor_State. 6.3.9 Attaching a link to an Aggregator — the port binds to a compatible Aggregator (matching operational System Identifier and operational Key, and whose other links chose the same LAG); non-aggregateable links model as a LAG of size one. 6.3.10 Signaling readiness to transfer user data — each side signals readiness only once it is sure no frames from a previous LAG remain in transit (possibly using the Marker protocol). 6.3.11 Enabling the Frame Collector and Frame Distributor — both start disabled; the Collector enables when a port is Collecting, and the Distributor enables only after the peer reports its Collector is up. 6.3.12 MAC_Operational status — the Aggregator presents MAC_Operational TRUE to higher layers when Aggregator_Operational is TRUE (at least one attached port Collecting and Distributing), reported via aAggOperState. 6.3.13 Monitoring membership — each link is watched so both ends still agree; a change can force re-selection. 6.3.14 Detaching a link — Collecting and Distributing are set FALSE, the port detaches, LACP tells the Partner, and another same-Key Aggregator can be chosen; the Marker protocol or Conversation-Sensitive Collection and Distribution with Discard Wrong Conversation (DWC) prevents reordering when a conversation moves. 6.3.15 Configuration and administrative control — management can read/change port Keys, Aggregator Keys, the 'incapable of aggregation' flag, and LACP parameters. 6.3.16 Control state information — per link, Control tracks its LAG ID, associated Aggregator, and the Collecting/Distributing booleans; per Aggregator, it tracks operational status and the list of selected/attached ports, all shared via state variables rather than a formal service interface.
blocks.zh: 沿着一条链路从冷启动到拆除，逐一标注准确的条款号：6.3.6 链路聚合组标识——一个 LAG 要么是共享同一系统对与运行键的可聚合链路，要么是一条孤立链路；LAG ID 由运行系统标识符、键以及（对孤立链路而言）端口标识符按 [(SKP),(TLQ)] 构造（6.3.6.1），并以规范十六进制约定书写（6.3.6.2）。6.3.7 选择 LAG——每个端口被放入其 LAG ID 所命名的 LAG；选择会短暂延迟以吸收对端信息，若无信息到达则回退到管理默认值。6.3.8 就 LAG 达成一致——帧流动前两端必须就 LAG ID 达成一致；对于已知的孤立链路，LACP 可通过清除 LACPDU Actor_State 中的 Aggregation 位来跳过此步。6.3.9 把链路挂接到聚合器——端口绑定到兼容聚合器（运行系统标识符与运行键匹配，且其上其他链路已选择同一 LAG）；不可聚合的链路被建模为成员数为一的 LAG。6.3.10 发出传输用户数据的就绪信号——只有当确信先前 LAG 的帧已无在途时，各端才发出就绪信号（可能借助标记协议）。6.3.11 启用帧收集器与帧分发器——两者初始均禁用；端口处于 Collecting 时收集器启用，分发器则要等对端报告其收集器已启用后才启用。6.3.12 MAC_Operational 状态——当 Aggregator_Operational 为 TRUE（至少一个挂接端口同时 Collecting 与 Distributing）时，聚合器向上层呈现 MAC_Operational 为 TRUE，并经 aAggOperState 上报。6.3.13 监控成员关系——持续监视每条链路以确保两端仍一致；变化可能迫使重新选择。6.3.14 脱离链路——把 Collecting 与 Distributing 置为 FALSE，端口脱离，LACP 通知伙伴，随后可选择另一个同键聚合器；当会话搬移时，标记协议或带丢弃错误会话（DWC，Discard Wrong Conversation）的会话敏感收集与分发可防止乱序。6.3.15 配置与管理控制——管理可读取/更改端口键、聚合器键、“不可聚合”标志以及 LACP 参数。6.3.16 控制状态信息——针对每条链路，控制功能跟踪其 LAG ID、关联聚合器以及 Collecting/Distributing 布尔量；针对每个聚合器，跟踪其运行状态及已选择/已挂接端口列表，全部通过共享状态变量而非正式服务接口交换。

blocks.en: The mental model to keep: identities (System Id, Aggregator id, Port Id, Key) decide which links may bundle; the control lifecycle (select -> agree -> attach -> signal ready -> enable) decides when they actually carry user data; and the data plane (Distributor -> ports -> Collector, gated by Distributing/Collecting and policed by CollectorMaxDelay) keeps each conversation on one link so nothing is reordered. Part 2 of this lesson opens up the LACP protocol itself — the LACPDU format and the state machines that drive these transitions.
blocks.zh: 需要牢记的心智模型：标识（系统标识、聚合器标识、端口标识、键）决定哪些链路可以捆绑；控制生命周期（选择 → 达成一致 → 挂接 → 发出就绪信号 → 启用）决定它们何时真正承载用户数据；而数据面（分发器 → 各端口 → 收集器，受 Distributing/Collecting 门控并由 CollectorMaxDelay 约束）则让每个会话固定在一条链路上，从而不发生乱序。本课的下篇将展开 LACP 协议本身——LACPDU 的格式以及驱动这些状态迁移的状态机。

cites.note: 6.1 Overview: goals (6.1.1), architectural positioning (6.1.2), Protocol Parser/Multiplexer (6.1.3)
6.2 operation: principles, service interfaces, Frame Collector/Distributor, Marker, Aggregator Parser/Multiplexer
6.3 Link Aggregation Control: identities and the full attach/detach lifecycle (6.3.1-6.3.16)
